name: Who Directed It?
description: See a famous film, name its director: 129 essentials from Citizen Kane and Casablanca to Parasite and Everything Everywhere All at Once. Spans Hollywood classics, New Hollywood, modern blockbusters, world cinema, and animation.

fields.extra: New Hollywood landmark. Coppola also directed The Conversation and Apocalypse Now.
Launched the biggest franchise in film history. Lucas also directed American Graffiti and THX 1138.
Won the Palme d'Or in 1994. Tarantino also directed Reservoir Dogs and Kill Bill.
Won 11 Oscars including Best Director. Cameron also directed The Terminator and Avatar.
Effectively invented the summer blockbuster. Spielberg also directed E.T. and Raiders of the Lost Ark.
Center of Nolan's Batman trilogy. He also directed Inception and Oppenheimer.
Released the same year as his Schindler's List. Spielberg also directed Jaws.
Adapted from a Stephen King novella. Darabont also directed The Green Mile.
Won Zemeckis the Best Director Oscar. He also directed Back to the Future and Cast Away.
Directed by Lana and Lilly Wachowski, credited on release as the Wachowski Brothers. They also directed Speed Racer and Cloud Atlas.

fields.front: Who directed Back to the Future (1985)?
fields.extra: Produced by Steven Spielberg. Zemeckis also directed Who Framed Roger Rabbit and Forrest Gump.
Spielberg also directed Close Encounters of the Third Kind and Jurassic Park.
Original heist thriller set inside dreams. Nolan also directed Memento and Interstellar.
Highest grossing film of all time. Cameron also directed Titanic and Aliens.

fields.front: Who directed The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001)?
fields.extra: Shot back to back with its sequels in Jackson's native New Zealand. He also directed King Kong (2005).
The master of suspense. Hitchcock also directed Vertigo, Rear Window, and The Birds.

fields.front: Who directed The Wizard of Oz (1939)?
fields.extra: Fleming took over mid-production and received sole credit. He also directed Gone with the Wind the same year.
Hungarian-born Hollywood craftsman. Curtiz also directed The Adventures of Robin Hood and White Christmas.
Welles co-wrote, directed, and starred at age 25. He also directed Touch of Evil.

fields.front: Who directed Gone with the Wind (1939)?
fields.extra: Fleming replaced George Cukor early in production. He also directed The Wizard of Oz the same year.
Co-written with Arthur C. Clarke. Kubrick also directed Dr. Strangelove and A Clockwork Orange.
Adapted from Stephen King, who famously disliked it. Kubrick also directed 2001: A Space Odyssey and Full Metal Jacket.
Won Spielberg his first Best Director Oscar. He also directed Saving Private Ryan.
Scorsese also directed Taxi Driver, Raging Bull, and The Departed.
Won the Palme d'Or in 1976. Scorsese also directed Raging Bull and Goodfellas.
Vietnam epic loosely based on Heart of Darkness. Coppola also directed The Godfather films.

fields.front: Who directed The Godfather Part II (1974)?
fields.extra: First sequel to win Best Picture. Coppola also directed The Conversation the same year.
Scott also directed Blade Runner and Gladiator.
Adapted from Philip K. Dick. Scott also directed Alien and The Martian.

fields.front: Who directed Raiders of the Lost Ark (1981)?
fields.extra: First Indiana Jones film, created with George Lucas. Spielberg also directed Jaws and E.T.

fields.front: Who directed The Silence of the Lambs (1991)?
fields.extra: Swept the top five Oscars. Demme also directed Philadelphia.
Adapted from Chuck Palahniuk. Fincher also directed Se7en and Zodiac.
Cameron also directed The Terminator, Aliens, and Titanic.
McTiernan also directed Predator and The Hunt for Red October.
Won Best Picture. Scott also directed Alien, Blade Runner, and Thelma and Louise.

fields.front: Who directed The Empire Strikes Back (1980)?
fields.extra: George Lucas handed the sequel to Kershner, his former USC film school teacher, while producing and writing the story.
Won Best Picture and earned Avildsen the Best Director Oscar. He also directed The Karate Kid.
First non-English-language film to win Best Picture. Bong also directed Memories of Murder and Snowpiercer.
Studio Ghibli feature that won the Best Animated Feature Oscar. Miyazaki also directed Princess Mononoke and Howl's Moving Castle.
First fully computer-animated feature film, made at Pixar. Lasseter also directed A Bug's Life.
Disney Renaissance musical. Minkoff later directed Stuart Little.
Topped the 2012 Sight and Sound critics poll. Hitchcock also directed Psycho and Notorious.
Hitchcock also directed Dial M for Murder and To Catch a Thief, both with Grace Kelly.
Crop duster chase and Mount Rushmore finale. Hitchcock also directed The 39 Steps.
Kubrick withdrew it from British release for decades. He also directed Barry Lyndon.
Cold War satire starring Peter Sellers in three roles. Kubrick also directed Paths of Glory and Lolita.

fields.front: Who directed One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest (1975)?
fields.extra: Swept the Big Five Oscars. Czech émigré Forman also directed Amadeus.
Friedkin also directed The French Connection, which won him the Best Director Oscar.
Neo-noir written by Robert Towne. Polanski also directed Rosemary's Baby and The Pianist.
Won Nichols the Best Director Oscar. He also directed Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf?
Won Best Picture over Star Wars. Allen also directed Manhattan and Midnight in Paris.
Won Best Picture and Best Director. Cimino's follow-up Heaven's Gate famously sank United Artists.
Black and white boxing biopic. Scorsese also directed Taxi Driver and The King of Comedy.
Written by Aaron Sorkin. Fincher also directed Fight Club and Gone Girl.

fields.front: Who directed No Country for Old Men (2007)?
fields.extra: Won the brothers Best Picture and Best Director. They also directed The Big Lebowski.
Both brothers directed, though the screen credit at the time named Joel alone. They also made No Country for Old Men.

fields.front: Who directed There Will Be Blood (2007)?
fields.extra: Oil epic starring Daniel Day-Lewis. Anderson also directed Boogie Nights and Magnolia.
Peele's directing debut won him the Original Screenplay Oscar. He also directed Us and Nope.
Tarantino also directed Pulp Fiction and Django Unchained.
Revisionist Western that won Eastwood Best Picture and Best Director. He also directed Million Dollar Baby.

fields.front: Who directed Do the Right Thing (1989)?
fields.extra: One hot day in Bed-Stuy, Brooklyn. Lee also directed Malcolm X and BlacKkKlansman.
Best Picture winner. Jenkins also directed If Beale Street Could Talk.
Best Picture winner from the British artist turned director. McQueen also directed Hunger and Shame.
Coogler also directed Fruitvale Station and Creed.

fields.front: Who directed Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind (2004)?
fields.extra: French music video visionary, written with Charlie Kaufman. Gondry also directed The Science of Sleep.
Chazelle also directed La La Land, which made him the youngest Best Director winner ever.

fields.front: Who directed The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014)?
fields.extra: Anderson also directed Rushmore, The Royal Tenenbaums, and Moonrise Kingdom.

fields.front: Who directed Mad Max: Fury Road (2015)?
fields.extra: Miller created Mad Max in 1979 and also directed Babe: Pig in the City and Happy Feet.
French-Canadian director. Villeneuve also directed Arrival and Blade Runner 2049.
Won Nolan his first Best Director Oscar. He also directed The Dark Knight trilogy.

fields.front: Who directed Everything Everywhere All at Once (2022)?
fields.extra: The directing duo known as Daniels swept the 2023 Oscars. They also made Swiss Army Man.

fields.front: Who directed Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone (2001)?
fields.extra: Columbus also directed Home Alone and Mrs. Doubtfire.
Won Best Picture and Best Director. Iñárritu also directed The Revenant and Amores Perros.
Bigelow became the first woman to win the Best Director Oscar. She also directed Point Break and Zero Dark Thirty.
Won Coppola the Original Screenplay Oscar. She also directed The Virgin Suicides and Marie Antoinette.
Gerwig's solo directing debut. She also directed Little Women and Barbie.
Highest grossing film of 2023. Gerwig also directed Lady Bird and Little Women.
Jenkins also directed Monster, which won Charlize Theron a Best Actress Oscar.
Martin Luther King Jr. drama. DuVernay also directed the documentary 13th.
Adapted from Bret Easton Ellis. Harron also directed I Shot Andy Warhol.
Won Zhao the Best Director Oscar. She also directed The Rider.
Palme d'Or winner. Campion later won the Best Director Oscar for The Power of the Dog.

fields.front: Who directed Singin' in the Rain (1952)?
fields.extra: Star Kelly co-directed with Donen; the pair also made On the Town together.

fields.front: Who directed Some Like It Hot (1959)?
fields.extra: Wilder also directed Sunset Boulevard and The Apartment.
Hollywood noir about a faded silent star. Wilder also directed Double Indemnity.

fields.front: Who directed It's a Wonderful Life (1946)?
fields.extra: Capra also directed Mr. Smith Goes to Washington and It Happened One Night.
Lumet's feature debut. He also directed Dog Day Afternoon and Network.
British epic specialist. Lean also directed The Bridge on the River Kwai and Doctor Zhivago.
Won a record 11 Oscars. Wyler also directed Roman Holiday and The Best Years of Our Lives.
Western master with a record four Best Director Oscars. Ford also directed Stagecoach and The Grapes of Wrath.
Real-time Western. Zinnemann also directed From Here to Eternity and A Man for All Seasons.
Kazan also directed A Streetcar Named Desire and East of Eden.
British noir set in postwar Vienna. Reed also directed Oliver!
Chaplin wrote, directed, scored, and starred. He also directed City Lights and The Great Dictator.
Towering figure of Japanese cinema. Kurosawa also directed Rashomon, Ikiru, and Ran.
Its Venice festival prize introduced Japanese cinema to the West. Kurosawa also directed Seven Samurai.
Japanese master of the quiet family drama. Ozu also directed Late Spring.
Whimsical Montmartre fable. Jeunet also directed Delicatessen and A Very Long Engagement.
Brazilian favela epic, co-directed by Kátia Lund. Meirelles also directed The Constant Gardener.
Spanish-language dark fantasy from the Mexican director. Del Toro also directed The Shape of Water.

fields.front: Who directed Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon (2000)?
fields.extra: Taiwanese director. Lee won Best Director Oscars for Brokeback Mountain and Life of Pi.
Korean revenge thriller that won the Grand Prix at Cannes. Park also directed The Handmaiden.

fields.front: Who directed In the Mood for Love (2000)?
fields.extra: Hong Kong master of longing. Wong also directed Chungking Express and Happy Together.
Black and white memoir of 1970s Mexico City. Cuarón also directed Children of Men and Gravity.
The Mexican debut that launched Iñárritu. He also directed Birdman and The Revenant.

fields.front: Who directed All About My Mother (1999)?
fields.extra: Spanish melodrama master. Almodóvar also directed Talk to Her and Volver.
Benigni directed and starred, winning the Best Actor Oscar.
Italian love letter to moviegoing that won the Foreign Language Oscar. Tornatore also directed Malèna.

fields.front: Who directed The Good, the Bad and the Ugly (1966)?
fields.extra: Spaghetti Western maestro. Leone also directed Once Upon a Time in the West.
Italian neorealist landmark. De Sica also directed Umberto D. and Shoeshine.
Italian master of the surreal. Fellini also directed La Dolce Vita and Amarcord.
French New Wave manifesto. Godard also directed Contempt and Pierrot le Fou.
French New Wave. Truffaut also directed Jules and Jim and Day for Night.
Godmother of the French New Wave. Varda also directed Vagabond and Faces Places.
Swedish master. Bergman also directed The Seventh Seal and Fanny and Alexander.
A knight plays chess with Death. Bergman also directed Persona and Wild Strawberries.
German Expressionist epic. Lang also directed M before fleeing to Hollywood.
Soviet montage pioneer, famous for the Odessa Steps sequence. Eisenstein also directed October.
Soviet master of the metaphysical. Tarkovsky also directed Solaris and Mirror.
First film of the Polish director's Three Colours trilogy. Kieślowski also made Dekalog and The Double Life of Veronique.
First Iranian film to win the Foreign Language Oscar. Farhadi also directed The Salesman.
Opens the Apu Trilogy from India's most celebrated director. Ray also directed The Music Room.

fields.front: Who directed Portrait of a Lady on Fire (2019)?
fields.extra: French period romance that won Best Screenplay at Cannes. Sciamma also directed Girlhood.

fields.front: Who directed The Lives of Others (2006)?
fields.extra: Stasi surveillance drama that won the Foreign Language Oscar. It was Donnersmarck's debut feature.
Pixar adventure co-directed by Bob Peterson. Docter also directed Monsters, Inc. and Inside Out.
Bird also directed The Iron Giant and Ratatouille.
Studio Ghibli icon whose title creature became the studio logo. Miyazaki also directed Spirited Away.
Cyberpunk anime landmark. Ōtomo adapted his own manga and also directed Steamboy.

fields.front: Who directed Grave of the Fireflies (1988)?
fields.extra: Studio Ghibli war tragedy. Takahata also directed The Tale of the Princess Kaguya.
